Large Language Models are neural networks trained on massive text datasets to understand language.
GPT stands for Generative Pre-trained Transformer, developed by OpenAI.
Claude is an AI assistant developed by Anthropic focused on safety and helpfulness.
BERT is a bidirectional transformer model developed by Google for NLP tasks.
Transformers use self-attention mechanisms to process entire sequences simultaneously.
Self-attention allows each token to attend to all other tokens in the sequence.
The attention mechanism computes relevance scores between all pairs of tokens.
Tokenization converts raw text into numerical tokens for language model processing.
BPE (Byte Pair Encoding) is a tokenization algorithm used by GPT models.
WordPiece is a tokenization algorithm used by BERT that splits unknown words into subwords.
Context window is the maximum number of tokens a language model can process at once.
Temperature controls the randomness of language model outputs, higher means more creative.
Top-p sampling selects tokens from the smallest set whose cumulative probability exceeds p.
Zero-shot learning allows models to perform tasks without any task-specific training examples.
Few-shot learning provides a small number of examples in the prompt to guide the model.
Chain-of-thought prompting encourages models to show reasoning steps before answering.
Hallucination occurs when a language model generates plausible but factually incorrect text.
RAG reduces hallucinations by grounding model responses in retrieved factual documents.
Hugging Face is a platform providing thousands of pre-trained models and datasets.
Sentence Transformers convert sentences into dense vector embeddings for semantic search.
FAISS enables fast approximate nearest neighbor search over millions of vectors.
Vector databases store and retrieve embeddings efficiently for RAG applications.
Fine-tuning adapts a pre-trained language model to a specific domain or task.
RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback) aligns LLMs with human preferences.
Prompt engineering designs effective inputs to elicit desired outputs from language models.
System prompts provide instructions and context to guide language model behavior.
LLM inference is the process of generating text from a trained language model.
Model quantization reduces model size by using lower precision numerical representations.
